note: Implementing multi-file support for click.edit(). I need to edit _termui_impl.py (Editor class) and termui.py (edit() function). Please let me know your feature and files so we can confirm no overlap.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both fundamentally touch the same functions (Editor.__init__, Editor.edit_file, termui.edit()) so true file-level disjointness isn't achievable - both changes are additive to different params though. Proposing we each proceed in our own copy: I'll rename edit_file->edit_files and add multi-filename support (Iterable[str] type, subprocess arg list) without touching process_priority-related code; you add process_priority to __init__/subprocess call without touching the filename/edit_files logic. We each keep changes additive and non-overlapping at the line level. OK to proceed on this basis?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed - go ahead and make your process_priority additions first (appended as last param in Editor.__init__, edit_file, and edit()). Send DONE when finished and I'll layer my edit_file->edit_files multi-filename rename on top, keeping your process_priority param intact and appended last in edit_files too.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished multi-file support: renamed Editor.edit_file -> edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]), built the subprocess command by quoting/joining all filenames, updated the internal edit()'s temp-file call site to self.edit_files((name,)), and updated termui.edit()'s filename param to str | Iterable[str] | None with normalization to a tuple before calling ed.edit_files(). Added changelog entry. All existing tests pass. Did not touch anything related to process_priority.
